children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The Commissioner, in consultation with the appropriate congressional committees, shall establish priorities and performance standards to measure the development and levels of achievement of the customs modernization, trade facilitation, and trade enforcement functions and programs described in subsection (b).

children.heading: Minimum priorities and standards
children.label: 2 Minimum priorities and standards
children.text: Such priorities and performance standards shall, at a minimum, include priorities and standards relating to efficiency, outcome, output, and other types of applicable measures.
Each of the priority trade issues described in .section 4322 of this title
The Centers of Excellence and Expertise described in .section 4317 of this title
Drawback for exported merchandise under section 313 of the Tariff Act of 1930 (), as amended by section 906 of this Act.19 U.S.C. 1313
Transactions relating to imported merchandise in bond.

Collection of countervailing duties assessed under subtitle A of title VII of the Tariff Act of 1930 ( et seq.) and antidumping duties assessed under subtitle B of title VII of the Tariff Act of 1930 ( et seq.).19 U.S.C. 167119 U.S.C. 1673
The issuance of regulations and rulings.
The issuance of Regulatory Audit Reports.

children.heading: Consultations
children.label: 1 Consultations
children.text: The consultations required by subsection (a)(1) shall occur, at a minimum, on an annual basis.

children.heading: Notification
children.label: 2 Notification
children.text: The Commissioner shall notify the appropriate congressional committees of any changes to the priorities or performance standards referred to in subsection (a) not later than 30 days before such changes are to take effect.
